sources.name: LMArena (Chatbot Arena)
sources.notes: Crowd-sourced pairwise Elo. 7,274,904 votes across 374 models as of 2026-07-10.

sources.name: Artificial Analysis
sources.notes: Intelligence Index v4.x composite. Set AA_API_KEY to enable API fetch.

sources.name: SWE-bench Verified / Pro
sources.notes: Verified: 500 human-reviewed GitHub issues. Pro: harder agentic-coding successor.

sources.name: Endor Labs Agent Security League
sources.notes: 200 real-world vulnerability-fix tasks across 108 projects and 77 CWE classes.

sources.name: Tau2-bench (Sierra)
sources.notes: Multi-turn tool-use agents in retail/airline/telecom customer service. 226 models ranked.

sources.name: Vending-Bench 2 (Andon Labs)
sources.notes: One simulated year running a vending business from a $500 float; score is final balance.

sources.name: GAIA (Princeton HAL)
sources.notes: General assistant tasks in the HAL scaffold; harness alone moves scores ~7 points.

sources.name: Cybench defensive subset (CoTool)
sources.notes: 18 defensive tasks from Cybench's 40 professional CTF challenges (HackTheBox, SekaiCTF, Glacier, HKCert).

models.name: Claude Mythos 5
models.note: Same model as Fable 5, safeguards lifted. Project Glasswing partners only.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.8
models.note: $5/$25 per MTok. Fable's designated fallback model.

models.name: GPT-5.6 Sol
models.note: OpenAI flagship; 'Ultra' variant scores higher on Terminal-Bench.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.note: Terminal-Bench score is the Codex CLI harness.

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.note: LiveCodeBench score is Gemini 3 Pro Preview.

models.name: DeepSeek V4 Pro
models.note: Top open-weights model in the LMArena frontier cluster.

models.name: GLM-5.2
models.note: HLE score is with tools (40.5 without).

models.name: Kimi K2.5
models.note: HLE score is with tools (31.5 without).

benchmarks.name: Tau2-bench
benchmarks.note: Multi-turn tool-use customer-service agents. Near saturation — leader at 99.1%.

benchmarks.name: Vending-Bench 2
benchmarks.note: Final balance after one simulated year of running a business from a $500 float.

benchmarks.name: GAIA (HAL)
benchmarks.note: General-assistant tasks. Harness choice alone moves the same model ~7 points.

benchmarks.name: ARC-AGI-2
benchmarks.note: Novel abstract reasoning; resistant to memorization.

benchmarks.name: Cybench (defensive subset)
benchmarks.note: 18 defensive tasks from 40 professional CTF challenges.

benchmarks.name: Endor ASL SecPass
benchmarks.note: Share of vulnerability fixes that pass security tests (not just functional tests).

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench Pro
benchmarks.note: Harder successor to Verified; the current agentic-coding discriminator.

benchmarks.name: SWE-bench Verified
benchmarks.note: OpenAI audit: 59.4% of the hardest unsolved problems have flawed test cases.

benchmarks.name: Terminal-Bench 2.1
benchmarks.note: Fable 5 was suspended 2026-06-12 and later reinstated.

benchmarks.name: AA Intelligence Index
benchmarks.note: v4.x — versions v4.0 and v4.1 report different absolute values.

benchmarks.name: GPQA Diamond
benchmarks.note: PhD-level science QA. Near saturation at the frontier.

scores.note: Leaderboard top score as of 2026-07-08; benchmark near saturation.
Score is predecessor Claude Opus 4.6 — current leader; only 4 models evaluated.
Predecessor Sonnet 4.5 leads the HAL scaffolded board; Anthropic holds the top six slots.
Predecessor Claude Opus 4.5; tied for the ceiling with GPT-5.2 at ~3x the cost per task.
League-wide SecPass had previously peaked at 17.3% while FuncPass climbed to 84.4%.
~97% of Opus 4.8's score at 60% of the price.
AA reports Fable 5 (with Opus fallback) as its highest-intelligence model; exact index differs between v4.0 and v4.1.

insights.title: Coding is measured. Planning isn't.
insights.body: Coding has four live, regularly refreshed leaderboards covering the whole frontier. Long-horizon planning has a 4-model sample (Vending-Bench 2) and a saturated tool-use board (Tau2's leader sits at 99.1%). The pillar everyone claims to care about has the weakest evidence base.

insights.title: Security scores collapse when it matters
insights.body: The frontier tops out at 61% on defensive CTF tasks — and when agents write actual vulnerability fixes, only 19% survive security tests (Fable 5, the current best). Over 80% of functionally-correct agent code ships with vulnerabilities. Passing CI is not passing security.

insights.title: Nobody has tested the current frontier
insights.body: Every Cybench and Vending-Bench entry is a predecessor generation — Opus 4.5/4.6, GPT-5.2, Gemini 3.0. Daggered (†) cells mark these proxies. The models you'd actually deploy today are unscored on two of the three pillars that matter.

insights.title: Each pillar has a different champion
insights.body: Coding belongs to Anthropic (Fable 5's 80.3% SWE-Pro) and OpenAI's terminal agents (Sol 88.8%). Best planning evidence points to the Anthropic lineage (Vending-Bench leader, top six GAIA slots). Security's ceiling is a 61% tie — where GPT-5.2 matched Opus 4.5 at a third of the cost.

insights.title: Why there's no single composite score
insights.body: Models are tested on different subsets — averaging Fable's SecPass against Opus's Cybench would compare different exams. We rank within each benchmark and refuse a blended number. The methodology page documents exactly what feeds each pillar.

insights.title: Harnesses move scores as much as models
insights.body: The same Claude Opus 4 scores 64.9% on GAIA in one harness and 57.6% in another — a 7-point swing from scaffolding alone. Terminal-Bench scores are model+harness pairs. Treat every agentic number as a system score, not a model score.

hall_of_fame.fix: Stopped echoing user-controlled paths in error responses; routed detail to server-side logging.
Added a 256 KB cap on compressed JWE payloads before decompression.
Stripped SVG/XML image types from trusted data URLs.
Separated Splash credentials from forwarded requests.

swe.anecdote: Stripe reportedly compressed months of engineering into days — a codebase-wide migration on a 50-million-line Ruby project completed in one day.

cyber.external_partner_finding: Fable 5's safeguards against harmful cyber queries were the most robust of any model tested, including Opus 4.8 and 4.7.
cyber.defensive_refusals: Zero safety refusals across all 200 security-relevant vulnerability-fix tasks (Endor Labs).
cyber.limitation: Static analysis only — cannot verify exploitability against live systems or deployed configurations (MindFort).
cyber.mythos_note: Mythos 5 — the same model with cyber safeguards lifted — is described as the strongest cybersecurity model in the world, restricted to Project Glasswing partners.
